steps.title: Graph the inequalities and points
steps.content: A calculator-forward shortcut is to graph $y\le 3x+1$ and $x-y>1$, then plot the four choices. The correct point is the only one in the overlap of the two shaded regions.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• A) $(-2,-1)$: $3x+1=-5$, so $-1\le -5$ is false. Eliminated.
  • B) $(-1,3)$: $3x+1=-2$, so $3\le -2$ is false. Eliminated.
  • C) $(1,5)$: $3x+1=4$, so $5\le 4$ is false. Eliminated.
  • D) $(2,-1)$: $3x+1=7$, so $-1\le 7$ is true; and $x-y=2-(-1)=3>1$ is true. Both hold.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
